verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family expressed in testis/sperm. Unlike IZUMO1 (single-pass TM), IZUMO4 lacks a TM domain and is a secreted Ig-superfamily protein, but it forms a stable complex with IZUMO1 on the sperm surface via disulfide/non-covalent association, contributing to the sperm-egg fusion machinery. Surface display is restricted to the germline lineage (mature sperm), so this is tissue-restricted contextual surface accessibility rather than a canonical anchored receptor.
